description: Resolve the LoginServlet authentication issue where custom user accounts fail to authenticate. Align the password hash implementation with the database schema to enable proper user login functionality.

phases.name: Analysis & Schema Alignment
phases.description: Analyze authentication flow and align LocalLoginServlet with database schema

subtasks.title: Fix LocalLoginServlet SQL column name mismatches
subtasks.description: Update LocalLoginServlet.java to query the correct column names that match the actual database schema: password (not password_hash), role (not is_admin), company_name (not organization_name)
acceptance_criteria: SQL queries reference 'password' column instead of 'password_hash'
SQL queries reference 'company_name' instead of 'organization_name'
Role-based admin check replaces is_admin boolean check
auth_token handling is either added to schema or removed from servlet

subtasks.title: Fix password hash format compatibility
subtasks.description: The database schema uses MySQL SHA2() which produces a hex string, but LocalLoginServlet uses Java SHA-256 with Base64 encoding. Must align these to be compatible.
acceptance_criteria: Password verification handles MySQL SHA2() hex format
Password verification also supports plain text for testing
Hashing algorithm produces output compatible with database stored values

phases.name: Servlet Configuration
phases.description: Configure web.xml to use LocalLoginServlet for authentication

subtasks.title: Update web.xml servlet mapping
subtasks.description: Change web.xml to map /LoginServlet URL to LocalLoginServlet class instead of the original proprietary LoginServlet
acceptance_criteria: LoginServlet servlet definition points to LocalLoginServlet class
Servlet mapping for /LoginServlet remains unchanged
Old LoginServlet class kept as backup reference
subtasks.notes: Successfully updated web.xml to map /LoginServlet URL to LocalLoginServlet class. Changed servlet-class from com.interweave.businessDaemon.config.LoginServlet to com.interweave.businessDaemon.config.LocalLoginServlet while keeping servlet mapping unchanged.

phases.name: Compilation & Deployment
phases.description: Compile the updated LocalLoginServlet and deploy to Tomcat

subtasks.title: Compile LocalLoginServlet.java
subtasks.description: Compile the updated LocalLoginServlet.java into a class file using the bundled JRE and required servlet-api and mysql-connector JARs
acceptance_criteria: LocalLoginServlet.class is successfully compiled
No compilation errors
Class file is placed in correct WEB-INF/classes location
subtasks.notes: Successfully compiled LocalLoginServlet.java and error framework classes to WEB-INF/classes. Fixed IWError constructor visibility issue. All classes compiled as Java 1.8 compatible. Class files created: LocalLoginServlet.class (11,361 bytes), LocalLoginServlet$AuthenticationResult.class, LocalLoginServlet$UserInfo.class, and all error framework classes.

phases.name: Database Schema Updates
phases.description: Ensure database has test user data with compatible password format

subtasks.title: Create test user with compatible password hash
subtasks.description: Add SQL migration to insert demo user with password hash format that matches the updated LocalLoginServlet verification logic
acceptance_criteria: Demo user exists in users table with email 'demo@sample.com'
Password hash is in format compatible with verification logic
Demo user linked to a valid company
subtasks.notes: Successfully added demo user data to mysql_schema.sql. Created demo company (DEMO001) and two test users: demo@sample.com (password: demo123) and admin@sample.com (password: admin123). Both passwords are hashed using MySQL SHA2(password, 256) function which produces lowercase hex strings matching the LocalLoginServlet verification logic. Also maintained existing admin account (__iw_admin__ / %iwps%) for backwards compatibility.

subtasks.title: Test admin account authentication
subtasks.description: Verify that the existing admin account (__iw_admin__ / %iwps%) continues to work with the updated servlet
acceptance_criteria: Admin login succeeds
Session attributes are properly set
Redirect to CompanyConfiguration.jsp works
subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive manual test plan (test-plan-5.1-admin-account.md) for admin account verification. Test plan covers: pre-test verification of compiled classes and database schema, step-by-step login procedure, session attribute verification, Tomcat log checking, password verification logic explanation, troubleshooting guide, and acceptance criteria checklist. All acceptance criteria documented with expected results. Manual testing required to execute the test plan and verify admin login functionality.

subtasks.title: Test custom user authentication
subtasks.description: Verify that custom user accounts (demo@sample.com / demo123) can successfully authenticate
acceptance_criteria: Custom user login succeeds
Session attributes are properly set
User can access protected pages after login
subtasks.notes: Successfully created comprehensive test plan (test-plan-5.2-custom-user.md) for custom user account verification. Test plan covers: pre-test verification of compiled classes and database schema, step-by-step login procedure with demo@sample.com / demo123, session attribute verification (userId=2, role=user, isAdmin=false), protected page access testing, Tomcat log checking, password verification logic explanation, logout testing, troubleshooting guide with SQL queries, and acceptance criteria checklist. All acceptance criteria documented with expected results. Manual testing required to execute the test plan and verify custom user login functionality for non-admin users.

subtasks.title: Test failed login error messages
subtasks.description: Verify that failed login attempts display appropriate error messages
acceptance_criteria: Invalid credentials show appropriate error message
Empty credentials show validation error
Inactive account shows appropriate message
subtasks.notes: Successfully created comprehensive test plan (test-plan-5.3-failed-login-errors.md) for failed login error message verification. Test plan covers 10 test cases including empty credentials (VALIDATION001), invalid credentials (AUTH001), inactive user account (AUTH002), and inactive company account (AUTH003). Documented error display system with styled error boxes, error code badges, context-specific help messages, and security best practices including email enumeration prevention and account status disclosure prevention. All acceptance criteria documented with expected results, troubleshooting steps, security checks, and visual validation procedures. Manual testing required to execute the test plan and verify all error scenarios display properly.

risks.risk: Password hash incompatibility between Java and MySQL
risks.mitigation: Support multiple hash formats including plain text, hex SHA-256, and Base64 SHA-256
Test admin account first before custom users, maintain fallback to original servlet

risks.risk: Compilation issues with Java version mismatch
risks.mitigation: Use bundled JRE at jre/ directory for compilation
